metadata.fixture: Streakly (synthetic SvelteKit habit tracker); base = faithful spec-first-project-setup output + design-explore DESIGN-HEURISTICS; base+overlay model
metadata.isolation: one general-purpose subagent per case, isolated cp -R workdir, skill read from SKILL.md (Skill tool not invoked), no dev server / npm / browser / network
contamination_controls: Synthetic project, fresh domain (not Recipe Box / not dweb).
Stack divergent from real repos (SvelteKit / Svelte stores / CSS vars).
Prompts never name the skill that should be routed to.

findings.title: spec-research uses inconsistent Status tokens for unlocked rows; template vocabulary disagrees with what code-agent keys on
findings.detail: Across 4 producer runs the draft Page-states rows were labelled with 4 different Status values: P1 'proposed', P2 'shipped', P3 'shipped', P4 'draft'. The feature-ideas.md template's Status example reads 'shipped / deferred / open'; spec-research's own SKILL examples use 'proposed'; code-agent's draft-vs-locked detection (C3) relies on recognizing a not-locked status. C3 passed only because its fixture hard-codes 'proposed' + DRAFT markers. If spec-research emits 'shipped' inside a DRAFT block (as P2/P3 did), a future code-agent run could misread unreviewed rows as ratified. Latent handoff bug.
findings.recommendation: Pin one Status vocabulary across the three artifacts: feature-ideas.md template, spec-research output format, and code-agent's gate. Suggest: proposed -> locked -> deferred. Update the template's Status example (currently 'shipped/deferred/open') to match, and have code-agent treat anything != 'locked' (and/or any live DRAFT marker) as not-implementable.
